title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Compiler Case: Inbox Retention Replay
summary: A consumer uses an in-memory set, marks messages complete before effects, reuses IDs across tenants, and assumes an idempotency key makes non-transactional email and payment calls exactly once. Planned artifact: Add and verify the inbox retention replay increment in durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.
Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Inbox Retention Replay · Theory Inbox Retention Replay
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3462 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
